title: Play, Transcultural: Interdisciplinary Game Studies
further_info: Recommended Reading Boellstorff, Tom, Bonnie Nardi, Celia Pearce, T.L. Taylor, et al., eds. 2012. Ethnography and Virtual Worlds: A Handbook of Method. Princeton: Princeton University Press. Calleja, Gordon. 2011. In-Game: From Immersion to Incorporation. Cambridge, Mass: MIT Press. Fernandez-Vara, Clara. 2015. Introduction to Game Analysis. New York: Routledge. Fine, Gary Alan. 1983. Shared Fantasy: Role-Playing Games as Social Worlds. Chicago: University of Chicago Press. Foucault, Michel. 1991. The Foucault Effect. Chicago: University of Chicago Press. Juneja, Monica. 2023. Can Art History Be Made Global? Meditations from the Periphery. Berlin, Boston: De Gruyter. Kamm, Bjorn-Ole. 2020. Role-Playing Games of Japan: Transcultural Dynamics and Orderings. New York: Palgrave Macmillan. Karabinus, Alisha, Carly A. Kocurek, Cody Mejeur, and Emma Vossen, eds. 2025. Historiographies of Game Studies: What It Has Been, What It Could Be. Earth, Milky Way: Punctum books. Latour, Bruno. 2005. Reassembling the Social. An Introduction to Actor-Network-Theory. Oxford: Oxford University Press. Law, John, and Annemarie Mol, eds. 2002. Complexities: Social Studies of Knowledge Practices. Durham: Duke University Press. Salen, Katie, and Eric Zimmerman. 2003. Rules of Play: Game Design Fundamentals. Cambridge: The MIT Press. Schatzki, Theodore R. 1996. Social Practices: A Wittgensteinian Approach to Human Activity and the Social. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press. Zagal, Jose Pablo, and Sebastian Deterding, eds. 2018 Role-Playing Game Studies: Transmedia Foundations. New York: Routledge. ATTENTION: Please note that this compact course is taking place in room 4400.01.12 (HCTS) on the following days: 17 April, 14:00-16:00 24 April, 16:00-18:00 25 April, 10-12, 13-15, 15:30-17:30 2 May, 10-12, 13-15, 15:30-17:30 8 May, 16:00-18:00 9 May, 10-12, 13-15, 15:30-17:30
